summary:
This file tells the system which files and folders should be left out of version control.

What that means in business terms:
- It prevents temporary, machine-generated, and sensitive files from being saved into the shared code history.
- It keeps the project cleaner and easier to manage.
- It reduces the risk of accidentally exposing private settings or cluttering the repository with files that do not belong there.

What gets ignored:
- Logs and error reports: files created when something goes wrong, used for troubleshooting but not meant to be stored permanently.
- Test coverage and build output: files created by testing or packaging the app, which can be recreated at any time.
- Dependency folders: large folders like `node_modules` and similar package caches, which are downloaded on each machine instead of being tracked.
- Environment files: files like `.env` that often contain passwords, API keys, or other private configuration. One example file is allowed in: `.env.example`, so teams can see the format without exposing real secrets.
- Tool caches and temporary files: leftovers from development tools such as Next.js, Nuxt, Gatsby, Svelte, Vite, Yarn, and others.
- Local editor settings and test artifacts: files used only on one developer’s computer or during local testing.

Visible outcome:
- Developers can work without accidentally committing junk or private data.
- The shared repository stays focused on the important source files.
- Builds and deployments are less likely to break because of unrelated local files.
- New team members can still see example configuration files, but not real secrets.

In short: this file is a housekeeping rulebook that keeps unnecessary, temporary, and sensitive files out of the project history.
raw_code_snapshot:
# Logs
logs
*.log
npm-debug.log*
yarn-debug.log*
yarn-error.log*
lerna-debug.log*

# Diagnostic reports (https://nodejs.org/api/report.html)
report.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.json

# Runtime data
pids
*.pid
*.seed
*.pid.lock

# Directory for instrumented libs generated by jscoverage/JSCover
lib-cov

# Coverage directory used by tools like istanbul
coverage
*.lcov

# nyc test coverage
.nyc_output

# Grunt intermediate storage (https://gruntjs.com/creating-plugins#storing-task-files)
.grunt

# Bower dependency directory (https://bower.io/)
bower_components

# node-waf configuration
.lock-wscript

# Compiled binary addons (https://nodejs.org/api/addons.html)
build/Release

# Dependency directories
node_modules/
jspm_packages/

# Snowpack dependency directory (https://snowpack.dev/)
web_modules/

# TypeScript cache
*.tsbuildinfo

# Optional npm cache directory
.npm

# Optional eslint cache
.eslintcache

# Optional stylelint cache
.stylelintcache

# Optional REPL history
.node_repl_history

# Output of 'npm pack'
*.tgz

# Yarn Integrity file
.yarn-integrity

# dotenv environment variable files
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
.diffdocrc
!.diffdocrc.example

# parcel-bundler cache (https://parceljs.org/)
.cache
.parcel-cache

# Next.js build output
.next
out

# Nuxt.js build / generate output
.nuxt
dist
.output

# Gatsby files
.cache/
# Comment in the public line in if your project uses Gatsby and not Next.js
# https://nextjs.org/blog/next-9-1#public-directory-support
# public

# vuepress build output
.vuepress/dist

# vuepress v2.x temp directory
.temp

# Sveltekit cache directory
.svelte-kit/

# vitepress build output
**/.vitepress/dist

# vitepress cache directory
**/.vitepress/cache

# Docusaurus cache and generated files
.docusaurus

# Serverless directories
.serverless/

# FuseBox cache
.fusebox/

# DynamoDB Local files
.dynamodb/

# Firebase cache directory
.firebase/

# TernJS port file
.tern-port

# Stores VSCode versions used for testing VSCode extensions
.vscode-test

# pnpm
.pnpm-store

# yarn v3
.pnp.*
.yarn/*
!.yarn/patches
!.yarn/plugins
!.yarn/releases
!.yarn/sdks
!.yarn/versions

# Vite files
vite.config.js.timestamp-*
vite.config.ts.timestamp-*
.vite/
.diffdoc/vectra/
